channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The 10 features of a successful PAM solution
item.description: Many sophisticated cyberattacks target elevated privileges so that the attackers can access to even more sensitive data—creating a compounding effect that organizations must protect against at all costs. It’s no wonder that securing privileged access is a foundational element of cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Beyond human identities: Cybersecurity’s blind spot in the age of AI agents
item.description: As AI continues to evolve and mature, organizations are beginning to deploy AI agents, which behave very differently from other forms of AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple and SpaceX enter the Star Wars era
item.description: Apple and SpaceX may be on a satellite communications collision course with Elon Musk’s firm attempting to block Apple from expanding satellite messaging for iPhones unless it does so with Starlink rather than other satellite communications partners.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Forget Samsung's new modular OLED panels – if this tech works on TVs we could get giant OLED TVs at half the price
item.description: Does this modular tech quietly fix the biggest problem in making huge OLED TVs? Only if it can overcome its own biggest flaw…
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: China's telecoms giant Huawei Technologies says its profit fell 28% while revenue surged in 2024
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Chinese telecoms gear giant Huawei said Monday its net profit fell 28% in 2024 while its revenue surged, as the company invested heavily in advanced technologies.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This may be the most private and secure cloud-based AI solution around
item.description: What if you could build a business-specific generative AI (genAI) solution that was hosted in the cloud? What if that cloud used the most energy-efficient systems in the world? And what if those services were also all running on the world’s most secure computing platform, the Mac? Wonder no more, b
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: These 5 new AI tools can help you do everything from managing tasks to improving your public speaking
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Hundreds of AI tools emerge every week. I’ve picked five new ones worth exploring.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: 6 advanced Gboard tricks for smarter Android typing
item.description: QWERTY, QWERTY, QWERTY. QWERTY. Oh — hi there! Sorry for the slightly nonsensical greeting. I’ve been thinking a lot about keyboards this week, y’see, and how that trusty ol’ QWERTY surface has evolved in our lives. Also, saying “QWERTY” over and over again is surprisingly fun to do.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As big tech circles, UK government struggles to reap promised AI benefits
item.description: The UK government’s grand plan for AI in the public sector is struggling in the face of growing technological challenges, a report by the Parliamentary Public Accounts Committee (PAC), a bipartisan group of elected members of parliament, has found.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kyndryl extends Google Cloud partnership for AI-based mainframe modernization
item.description: Kyndryl and Google Cloud are expanding their partnership to help customers use generative AI to move data off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vibe coding is groovy
item.description: Vibe coding is most definitely having a moment. Don’t feel bad if you haven’t heard of it—the Wikipedia page for it just went up on March 15 of this year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle releases ML-optimized GraalVM for JDK 24
item.description: Oracle has released GraalVM for JDK 24, an alternative Java Development Kit tuned to just-released JDK 24 that uses ML (machine learning)-based profile inference to boost peak performance by about 7.9% on average on microservices benchmarks, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise targets IoT, industrial networks with private 5G package
item.description: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise (ALE) has partnered with Celona to offer a turnkey private 5G package for industrial or IoT networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5
